response.justification: Aisha is a seasoned full-stack engineer with strong TypeScript/Node.js depth and genuine staff-level breadth, and the current role at re:cinq does touch MCP server development and agentic workflow infrastructure. However, the role demands 8+ years of production-grade AI delivery, and the resume shows that AI/GenAI work began only in April 2025 (~13 months ago). Python — the primary language for the AI ecosystem — is entirely absent from the skills section and work history. There is no mention of RAG architectures, vector databases, LLM evaluation pipelines, or specific multi-agent frameworks (LangGraph, CrewAI, etc.), all of which are central to this position. The gap between a strong generalist software engineering background and the deep, specialised GenAI delivery profile this role requires is too wide to bridge at the Staff level.
strengths: 15+ years of professional software engineering with multiple Staff-level titles, demonstrating sustained technical leadership and mentoring capacity.
Active hands-on work with Model Context Protocol (MCP) code generators and agentic workflow infrastructure in the current role, showing real entry into the agent-tooling space.

Proven experience with high-throughput data systems (millions of HTTP requests/second at Dunnhumby) and cloud infrastructure (AWS CDK at Optilyz), providing a credible foundation for AI infrastructure work.
concerns: Python is entirely absent from the skills list and all employment history; it is the de-facto language for AI/ML tooling, SDKs, and the broader GenAI ecosystem this role depends on.
No evidence of RAG architectures, vector databases, LLM evaluation or observability frameworks, or named multi-agent libraries — the core technical stack of the role is unrepresented.

GenAI experience is limited to roughly 13 months and described only in general terms ('agentic workflows', 'MCP frameworks') with no shipped production AI systems, customer engagements, or measurable outcomes cited.

key_factors.factor: MCP + agentic tooling in current role
